text: <par>Note the interesting superficial resemblance to Bodo <latinform>tapila</latinform> (114708).</par>
<par>< Thai <latinform>nâa-ʔòk</latinform> ‘chest’ . See also the Mpi form <latinform>ʔoʔ⁶-liʔ⁴ </latinform>‘chest’ (61975).</par>
<par>The last element <latinform>láŋ</latinform> in this form means 'water'; cf. <xref ref="191">#191</xref>, below.</par>
<par>The last element <latinform>láŋ</latinform> in this form means 'water'; cf. <xref ref="191">#191</xref>, below.</par>
<par>The first syllable is from 479 <reconstruction>*liŋ</reconstruction>, but *l- > n- is unexplained.</par>

<par>Note that Beaudouin glosses this form as 'tibia', but <latinform>mɛ-hnɨ</latinform> (with different tone on first morpheme) is 'eye', which suggests the long form should mean 'ankle', not 'tibia'. (Note Bradley has <latinform>mɛ̀-hnɨ</latinform> 'eye' and LTSR has <latinform>mɛ̀-hnɯ̄</latinform> 'eye'.)</par>

<par>Bisu <latinform>sàj</latinform> is a borrowing from Thai <latinform>sǎaj</latinform> 'cord, string'. This morpheme, interestingly enough, occurs in reduced form in the Thai word for 'navel', <latinform>sədɨɨ</latinform> (cf. Li Fang Kuei 1977:92).</par>
